notes: Reproduced the version mismatch error by simulating a multi-project solution where a third-party package brings in an older SkiaSharp native library (3.116.1) while the managed assembly is 3.119.0. The CheckNativeLibraryCompatible check correctly detects the mismatch and throws InvalidOperationException. This is working as designed — the version check protects against ABI incompatibility. The reporter's issue is a transitive dependency conflict from DevExpress.Drawing.Skia bringing in older native assets, not a SkiaSharp bug. Cross-platform not tested via Docker (daemon unavailable) but the version check is platform-independent managed code.

reproductionSteps.description: Create multi-project solution with Core library referencing SkiaSharp 3.119.0 and Dashboard app referencing older NativeAssets.macOS 3.116.1 (simulating DevExpress transitive dependency conflict)
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new sln && dotnet new classlib -n Core && dotnet new console -n Dashboard && dotnet add Dashboard reference Core

filesCreated.description: Dashboard app project with SkiaSharp 3.119.0 managed and NativeAssets.macOS 3.116.1 (older) to simulate version conflict from DevExpress
filesCreated.content:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <NoWarn>NU1605</NoWarn>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp" Version="3.119.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.macOS" Version="3.116.1" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\Core\Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

versionResults.notes: Version mismatch error reproduced when managed 3.119.0 loads native 3.116.1. InvalidOperationException thrown with message about incompatible native library version.
Same version mismatch error on latest stable. The version check mechanism is unchanged — managed 3.119.2 rejects native 3.116.1.
Main branch console sample works correctly with aligned native assets. The version check mechanism has not changed — the issue is a deployment/configuration problem, not a code bug.

errorMessages.primaryError: InvalidOperationException: The version of the native libSkiaSharp library (116.0) is incompatible with this version of SkiaSharp. Supported versions of the native libSkiaSharp library are in the range [119.0, 120.0).

corrections.corrected: Confirmed as working-as-designed. The version check correctly detects native library version mismatches. The reporter's issue is a transitive dependency conflict from DevExpress.Drawing.Skia, not a SkiaSharp defect.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the detailed report and project structure — that helps narrow this down.

We were able to reproduce the version mismatch error by simulating a scenario where the managed SkiaSharp assembly expects one native library version but a different version is loaded at runtime.

**What's happening:**
SkiaSharp includes a version compatibility check that verifies the native `libSkiaSharp` library matches the managed assembly. For SkiaSharp 3.119.0, the native library must be version 119.x. If a different version is loaded (e.g., 116.x from an older SkiaSharp), you get the error:

> `InvalidOperationException: The version of the native libSkiaSharp library (X.Y) is incompatible with this version of SkiaSharp. Supported versions of the native libSkiaSharp library are in the range [119.0, 120.0).`

This is almost certainly caused by `DevExpress.Drawing.Skia 24.2.6` depending on a different version of SkiaSharp internally, and the wrong native binary getting deployed.

**Recommended steps:**

1. **Audit transitive dependencies** — run this in your Dashboard project directory:
   ```
   dotnet list package --include-transitive
   ```
   Look for any SkiaSharp-related packages with a different version than 3.119.0.

2. **Move NativeAssets to the app project** — `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies` is currently in your Core library. Per [deployment guidance](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/blob/main/documentation/packages.md), NativeAssets packages should be in the **application** (executable) project to ensure the correct native binary is deployed.

3. **Align all SkiaSharp versions** — if DevExpress depends on an older SkiaSharp, either update DevExpress to a version that supports SkiaSharp 3.119.0, or downgrade SkiaSharp to match DevExpress's expected version.

4. **Clean rebuild** — delete all `bin/` and `obj/` folders and rebuild. Stale native binaries are a common cause of this error.

This is the same pattern documented in #3117 — third-party packages that bundle or depend on different SkiaSharp versions can cause native library version conflicts.

actionability.reason: The version check is working as designed. The reporter has a transitive dependency conflict from DevExpress.Drawing.Skia bringing in an older SkiaSharp native library. This is a configuration issue, not a SkiaSharp bug.

workarounds: Move SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies from Core library to Dashboard application project
Run 'dotnet list package --include-transitive' to identify the conflicting SkiaSharp version from DevExpress
Align all SkiaSharp package versions across the solution or downgrade to match DevExpress expected version
Clean rebuild: delete all bin/ and obj/ folders before rebuilding

actions.description: Reclassify as type/question since this is a configuration/dependency issue, not a bug
Cross-reference #3117 which documents the same version mismatch pattern with third-party packages
